{{日本の寺院 |名称 = 滝山寺 |image = [[File:Takisan-ji Temple Hondou, Yamagomori Taki-cho Okazaki 2022.jpg|270px]]
本堂（重要文化財）
（[[2022nd year ]]1月） |所在地 = [[愛知県]][[岡崎市]][[滝町 (岡崎市)|滝町]]山籠107 |位置 = {{Coord|34|59|17.37|N|137|12|20.26|E|region:JP-23_scale:20000_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=滝山寺}} |山号 = 吉祥陀羅尼山 |院号 = 薬樹王院 |宗派 = [[天台宗]] |本尊 = [[薬師如来]] |創建年 = 伝・[[Emperor Tenmu]]時代（[[673rd year ]] - [[686年]]） |開基 = 伝・[[役小角]] |別称 = |正式名 = 吉祥陀羅尼山藥樹王院瀧山寺 |文化財 = 本堂、三門、木造観音菩薩立像及梵天・帝釈天立像（重要文化財） |地図2 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|lat_deg=34|lat_min=59|lat_sec=17.37|lon_deg=137|lon_min=12|lon_sec=20.26|label=滝山寺|mark=Japanese Map symbol (Temple).svg|marksize=11|position=center|width=200|float=center}} |Name = Takisan-ji|image = ]][[File:Takisan-ji Temple Hondou, Yamagomori Taki-cho Okazaki 2022.jpg|270px]]
Main Hall (Important Cultural Property)
(]][[2022]] January)|Location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okazaki|qq=}}{{ill|Taki-cho|ja|滝町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taki-cho|qq=}} Yamagomori 107|Coordinates = {{Coord|34|59|17.37|N|137|12|20.26|E|region:JP-23_scale:20000_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=滝山寺}}|Mountain Name = Kisshōdarani-zan|Temple Name = Yakujūō-in|Sect = {{ill|Tendai Sect|ja|天台宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tendai Sect|qq=}}|Main image = {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師如来|1=Bhaisajyaguru|lt=Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakushi Nyorai|13=User:Immanuelle/Bhaisajyaguru|qq=}}|Founded = Traditionally during the era of Emperor Tenmu (673-686)|Founder = Traditionally {{ill|En no Gyōja|ja|役小角|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/En no Gyōja|qq=}}|Alternate Names = |Official Name = 吉祥陀羅尼山藥樹王院瀧山寺|Cultural Properties = Main Hall, Sanmon Gate, Wooden Standing Statue of Kannon Bosatsu, and Standing Statues of Brahma and Indra (Important Cultural Properties)|Map = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|lat_deg=34|lat_min=59|lat_sec=17.37|lon_deg=137|lon_min=12|lon_sec=20.26|label=Takisan-ji|mark=Japanese Map symbol (Temple).svg|marksize=11|position=center|width=200|float=center}}}}'''Takisan-ji''' (滝山寺) is a {{ill|Tendai Sect|ja|天台宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tendai Sect|qq=}} Buddhist temple located in {{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okazaki|qq=}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefecture|qq=}}. == Overview == === History === The mountain name is Kisshōdarani-zan, and the temple name is Yakujūō-in. The temple is renowned for the {{ill|Takisanji Oni Festival|ja|滝山寺鬼まつり|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takisanji Oni Festival|qq=}}.{{ill|Takisan Tōshō-gū|ja|滝山東照宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takisan Tōshō-gū|qq=}} is located to the east, and the {{ill|Hiyoshi Sannōsha Main Hall|ja|滝山東照宮#日吉山王社本殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiyoshi Sannōsha Main Hall|qq=}} is to the north of the Main Hall.According to the "Takisan-ji Engi" (a historical record of the temple; existing manuscripts are from the early modern period), the temple was established during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara period|qq=}} by order of Emperor Tenmu. {{ill|En no Gyōja|ja|役小角|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/En no Gyōja|qq=}}, a legendary ascetic, enshrined a golden statue of {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師如来|1=Bhaisajyaguru|lt=Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakushi Nyorai|13=User:Immanuelle/Bhaisajyaguru|qq=}} found in the {{ill|Aoki River|ja|青木川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoki River|qq=}}. The temple, initially named "Kisshō-ji," was later abandoned and fell into ruin. En no Gyōja is associated with numerous temples across Japan, especially those related to mountain and water source worship. Takisan-ji is believed to have served as a center for mountain worship.In the {{ill|Hōan era|ja|保安 (元号)|lt=Hōan era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōan era|qq=}} (1120–1123), the temple was revived by Buddhist monk Eikyū (永救) of the {{ill|Tendai Sect|ja|天台宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tendai Sect|qq=}} with support from the local Mononobe clan. Eikyū built the Main Hall and secured patronage from the Fujiwara family, increasing the temple's influence. Eikyū, a monk from {{ill|Kaga Province|ja|加賀国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaga Province|qq=}}, was trained at {{ill|Mount Hiei|ja|比叡山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hiei|qq=}} before spreading the Tendai teachings in {{ill|Mikawa Province|ja|三河国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikawa Province|qq=}}.From the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian period|qq=}} to the early {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura period|qq=}}, head priest Kanden (寛伝, 1142–1205) strengthened the temple's ties with powerful figures such as {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoritomo|qq=}}, ensuring support from the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|ja|鎌倉幕府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura shogunate|qq=}}.{{Cite journal|和書 |author=Hira Masayuki |year=2017 |title=熱田大宮司家の寛伝僧都と源頼朝 ―瀧山寺・日光山・高野大鐘― |journal=人間文化研究 |volume=38 |pages=288-243 |url=http://id.nii.ac.jp/1455/00001180/}} Statues crafted by {{ill|Unkei|ja|運慶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unkei|qq=}} and {{ill|Tankei|ja|湛慶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tankei|qq=}} were created during this period, commemorating Yoritomo.戸澤剛{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.bunsin.org/三河ビュー/三河の文化を訪ねて-人物史/運慶作三尊像を滝山寺に誘った%e3%80%80式部僧都%e3%80%80寛伝/ |title=運慶作三尊像を滝山寺に誘った　式部僧都　寛伝 |publisher=愛知教育文化振興会 |accessdate=2019-5-23 }}During the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi period|qq=}}, the temple was supported by the {{ill|Ashikaga clan|ja|足利氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga clan|qq=}}. However, its influence waned during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}} due to shifting political priorities.[http://jlogos.com/ausp/word.html?id=7354185 阿知和郷(中世)]角川地名大辞典（旧地名）Under {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|ja|徳川家康|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa Ieyasu|qq=}}, the temple regained support and received 412 koku in stipends. {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa Iemitsu|qq=}} later established the adjacent Takisan Tōshō-gū.{{Cite book|和書|author= |year=1993-3-15|title=新編 岡崎市史 総集編 20|publisher=新編岡崎市史編さん委員会|page=231-232}}After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Restoration|qq=}}, the temple lost its landholdings and shRank in scale. Today, it continues as a significant cultural and religious site. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall: A 14th-century structure exemplifying Esoteric Buddhist architecture.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/kenzoubutu/kunisitei/0006.html |title=瀧山寺本堂 |publisher=愛知県 |accessdate=2013-05-31}}* Sanmon Gate: Dating to the Kamakura period, though stylistically later.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/kenzoubutu/kunisitei/0004.html |title=瀧山寺三門 |publisher=愛知県 |accessdate=2019-05-23}}* Wooden statues of Kannon Bosatsu, Brahma, and Indra: Created by Unkei and Tankei in 1201.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/choukoku/kunisitei/0306.html |title=木造観音菩薩・梵天・帝釈天立像 |publisher=愛知県 |accessdate=2013-05-31}} === Prefectural and Municipal Cultural Properties === Additional wooden statues, ritual implements, and documents are designated at the prefectural and municipal levels. == Gallery == {{Gallery|title=|width=200|height=150|lines=|File:Okazaki-Takisanji-10.jpg|Sanmon Gate (Taisho era)|File:Okazaki-Takisanji-6.jpg|Scene from Oni Festival (2018)|File:Okazaki-Takisanji-7.jpg|Scene from Oni Festival (2019)|File:Hiyoshi-Sannosha-Hoden-1.jpg|Hiyoshi Sannōsha Main Hall (2021)}} == Access == * From {{ill|Higashi Okazaki Station|ja|東岡崎駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Okazaki Station|qq=}}, take a Meitetsu bus to "Takisanji-shita" stop. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://takisanji.net Takisan-ji Official Website] {{translated page|ja|滝山寺|version=102899477|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Takisanji]] [[Category:Temples in Okazaki]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Tendai temples]] [[Category:Temples dedicated to Yakushi Nyorai]] [[Category:Prefectural cultural properties in Aichi]] [[Category:Takisanji Temple]] }} [[sn:Takisanji]] [[ja:滝山寺]]

The temple, initially named "Kisshō-ji," was later abandoned and fell into ruin. En no Gyōja is associated with numerous temples across Japan, especially those related to mountain and water source worship. Takisan-ji is believed to have served as a center for mountain worship.In the {{ill|Hōan era|ja|保安 (元号)|lt=Hōan era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōan era|qq=}} (1120–1123), the temple was revived by Buddhist monk Eikyū (永救) of the {{ill|Tendai Sect|ja|天台宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tendai Sect|qq=}} with support from the local Mononobe clan. Eikyū built the Main Hall and secured patronage from the Fujiwara family, increasing the temple's influence. Eikyū, a monk from {{ill|Kaga Province|ja|加賀国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaga Province|qq=}}, was trained at {{ill|Mount Hiei|ja|比叡山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hiei|qq=}} before spreading the Tendai teachings in {{ill|Mikawa Province|ja|三河国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikawa Province|qq=}}.From the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian period|qq=}} to the early {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura period|qq=}}, head priest Kanden (寛伝, 1142–1205) strengthened the temple's ties with powerful figures such as {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoritomo|qq=}}, ensuring support from the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|ja|鎌倉幕府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura shogunate|qq=}}.{{Cite journal|和書 |author=Hira Masayuki |year=2017 |title=熱田大宮司家の寛伝僧都と源頼朝 ―瀧山寺・日光山・高野大鐘― |journal=人間文化研究 |volume=38 |pages=288-243 |url=http://id.nii.ac.jp/1455/00001180/}} Statues crafted by {{ill|Unkei|ja|運慶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unkei|qq=}} and {{ill|Tankei|ja|湛慶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tankei|qq=}} were created during this period, commemorating Yoritomo.戸澤剛{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.bunsin.org/三河ビュー/三河の文化を訪ねて-人物史/運慶作三尊像を滝山寺に誘った%e3%80%80式部僧都%e3%80%80寛伝/ |title=運慶作三尊像を滝山寺に誘った　式部僧都　寛伝 |publisher=愛知教育文化振興会 |accessdate=2019-5-23 }}During the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi period|qq=}}, the temple was supported by the {{ill|Ashikaga clan|ja|足利氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga clan|qq=}}. However, its influence waned during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}} due to shifting political priorities.[http://jlogos.com/ausp/word.html?id=7354185 阿知和郷(中世)]角川地名大辞典（旧地名）Under {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|ja|徳川家康|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa Ieyasu|qq=}}, the temple regained support and received 412 koku in stipends. {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa Iemitsu|qq=}} later established the adjacent Takisan Tōshō-gū.{{Cite book|和書|author= |year=1993-3-15|title=新編 岡崎市史 総集編 20|publisher=新編岡崎市史編さん委員会|page=231-232}}After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Restoration|qq=}}, the temple lost its landholdings and shRank in scale. Today, it continues as a significant cultural and religious site. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall: A 14th-century structure exemplifying Esoteric Buddhist architecture.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/kenzoubutu/kunisitei/0006.html |title=瀧山寺本堂 |publisher=愛知県 |accessdate=2013-05-31}}* Sanmon Gate: Dating to the Kamakura period, though stylistically later.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/kenzoubutu/kunisitei/0004.html |title=瀧山寺三門 |publisher=愛知県 |accessdate=2019-05-23}}* Wooden statues of Kannon Bosatsu, Brahma, and Indra: Created by Unkei and Tankei in 1201.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/choukoku/kunisitei/0306.html |title=木造観音菩薩・梵天・帝釈天立像 |publisher=愛知県 |accessdate=2013-05-31}} === Prefectural and Municipal Cultural Properties === Additional wooden statues, ritual implements, and documents are designated at the prefectural and municipal levels. == Gallery == {{Gallery|title=|width=200|height=150|lines=|File:Okazaki-Takisanji-10.jpg|Sanmon Gate (Taisho era)|File:Okazaki-Takisanji-6.jpg|Scene from Oni Festival (2018)|File:Okazaki-Takisanji-7.jpg|Scene from Oni Festival (2019)|File:Hiyoshi-Sannosha-Hoden-1.jpg|Hiyoshi Sannōsha Main Hall (2021)}} == Access == * From {{ill|Higashi Okazaki Station|ja|東岡崎駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Okazaki Station|qq=}}, take a Meitetsu bus to "Takisanji-shita" stop. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://takisanji.net Takisan-ji Official Website] {{translated page|ja|滝山寺|version=102899477|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Temples in Okazaki]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Tendai temples]] [[Category:Temples dedicated to Yakushi Nyorai]] [[Category:Prefectural cultural properties in Aichi]] [[Category:Takisanji Temple]] }} [[sn:Takisanji]] [[ja:滝山寺]]

本堂（重要文化財）
（[[2022nd year ]]1月） |所在地 = [[愛知県]][[岡崎市]][[滝町 (岡崎市)|滝町]]山籠107 |位置 = {{Coord|34|59|17.37|N|137|12|20.26|E|region:JP-23_scale:20000_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=滝山寺}} |山号 = 吉祥陀羅尼山 |院号 = 薬樹王院 |宗派 = [[天台宗]] |本尊 = [[薬師如来]] |創建年 = 伝・[[Emperor Tenmu]]時代（[[673rd year ]] - [[686年]]） |開基 = 伝・[[役小角]] |別称 = |正式名 = 吉祥陀羅尼山藥樹王院瀧山寺 |文化財 = 本堂、三門、木造観音菩薩立像及梵天・帝釈天立像（重要文化財） |地図2 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|lat_deg=34|lat_min=59|lat_sec=17.37|lon_deg=137|lon_min=12|lon_sec=20.26|label=滝山寺|mark=Japanese Map symbol (Temple).svg|marksize=11|position=center|width=200|float=center}} |Name = Takisan-ji|image = ]][[File:Takisan-ji Temple Hondou, Yamagomori Taki-cho Okazaki 2022.jpg|270px]]
Main Hall (Important Cultural Property)
(]][[2022]] January)|Location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okazaki|qq=}}{{ill|Taki-cho|ja|滝町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taki-cho|qq=}} Yamagomori 107|Coordinates = {{Coord|34|59|17.37|N|137|12|20.26|E|region:JP-23_scale:20000_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=滝山寺}}|Mountain Name = Kisshōdarani-zan|Temple Name = Yakujūō-in|Sect = {{ill|Tendai Sect|ja|天台宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tendai Sect|qq=}}|Main image = {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師如来|1=Bhaisajyaguru|lt=Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakushi Nyorai|13=User:Immanuelle/Bhaisajyaguru|qq=}}|Founded = Traditionally during the era of Emperor Tenmu (673-686)|Founder = Traditionally {{ill|En no Gyōja|ja|役小角|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/En no Gyōja|qq=}}|Alternate Names = |Official Name = 吉祥陀羅尼山藥樹王院瀧山寺|Cultural Properties = Main Hall, Sanmon Gate, Wooden Standing Statue of Kannon Bosatsu, and Standing Statues of Brahma and Indra (Important Cultural Properties)|Map = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|lat_deg=34|lat_min=59|lat_sec=17.37|lon_deg=137|lon_min=12|lon_sec=20.26|label=Takisan-ji|mark=Japanese Map symbol (Temple).svg|marksize=11|position=center|width=200|float=center}}}}'''Takisan-ji''' (滝山寺) is a {{ill|Tendai Sect|ja|天台宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tendai Sect|qq=}} Buddhist temple located in {{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okazaki|qq=}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefecture|qq=}}. == Overview == === History === The mountain name is Kisshōdarani-zan, and the temple name is Yakujūō-in. The temple is renowned for the {{ill|Takisanji Oni Festival|ja|滝山寺鬼まつり|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takisanji Oni Festival|qq=}}.{{ill|Takisan Tōshō-gū|ja|滝山東照宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takisan Tōshō-gū|qq=}} is located to the east, and the {{ill|Hiyoshi Sannōsha Main Hall|ja|滝山東照宮#日吉山王社本殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiyoshi Sannōsha Main Hall|qq=}} is to the north of the Main Hall.According to the "Takisan-ji Engi" (a historical record of the temple; existing manuscripts are from the early modern period), the temple was established during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara period|qq=}} by order of Emperor Tenmu. {{ill|En no Gyōja|ja|役小角|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/En no Gyōja|qq=}}, a legendary ascetic, enshrined a golden statue of {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師如来|1=Bhaisajyaguru|lt=Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakushi Nyorai|13=User:Immanuelle/Bhaisajyaguru|qq=}} found in the {{ill|Aoki River|ja|青木川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoki River|qq=}}. The temple, initially named "Kisshō-ji," was later abandoned and fell into ruin. En no Gyōja is associated with numerous temples across Japan, especially those related to mountain and water source worship. Takisan-ji is believed to have served as a center for mountain worship.In the {{ill|Hōan era|ja|保安 (元号)|lt=Hōan era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōan era|qq=}} (1120–1123), the temple was revived by Buddhist monk Eikyū (永救) of the {{ill|Tendai Sect|ja|天台宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tendai Sect|qq=}} with support from the local Mononobe clan. Eikyū built the Main Hall and secured patronage from the Fujiwara family, increasing the temple's influence. Eikyū, a monk from {{ill|Kaga Province|ja|加賀国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaga Province|qq=}}, was trained at {{ill|Mount Hiei|ja|比叡山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hiei|qq=}} before spreading the Tendai teachings in {{ill|Mikawa Province|ja|三河国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikawa Province|qq=}}.From the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian period|qq=}} to the early {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura period|qq=}}, head priest Kanden (寛伝, 1142–1205) strengthened the temple's ties with powerful figures such as {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoritomo|qq=}}, ensuring support from the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|ja|鎌倉幕府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura shogunate|qq=}}.{{Cite journal|和書 |author=Hira Masayuki |year=2017 |title=熱田大宮司家の寛伝僧都と源頼朝 ―瀧山寺・日光山・高野大鐘― |journal=人間文化研究 |volume=38 |pages=288-243 |url=http://id.nii.ac.jp/1455/00001180/}} Statues crafted by {{ill|Unkei|ja|運慶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unkei|qq=}} and {{ill|Tankei|ja|湛慶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tankei|qq=}} were created during this period, commemorating Yoritomo.戸澤剛{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.bunsin.org/三河ビュー/三河の文化を訪ねて-人物史/運慶作三尊像を滝山寺に誘った%e3%80%80式部僧都%e3%80%80寛伝/ |title=運慶作三尊像を滝山寺に誘った　式部僧都　寛伝 |publisher=愛知教育文化振興会 |accessdate=2019-5-23 }}During the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi period|qq=}}, the temple was supported by the {{ill|Ashikaga clan|ja|足利氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga clan|qq=}}. However, its influence waned during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}} due to shifting political priorities.[http://jlogos.com/ausp/word.html?id=7354185 阿知和郷(中世)]角川地名大辞典（旧地名）Under {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|ja|徳川家康|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa Ieyasu|qq=}}, the temple regained support and received 412 koku in stipends. {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa Iemitsu|qq=}} later established the adjacent Takisan Tōshō-gū.{{Cite book|和書|author= |year=1993-3-15|title=新編 岡崎市史 総集編 20|publisher=新編岡崎市史編さん委員会|page=231-232}}After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Restoration|qq=}}, the temple lost its landholdings and shRank in scale. Today, it continues as a significant cultural and religious site. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall: A 14th-century structure exemplifying Esoteric Buddhist architecture.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/kenzoubutu/kunisitei/0006.html |title=瀧山寺本堂 |publisher=愛知県 |accessdate=2013-05-31}}* Sanmon Gate: Dating to the Kamakura period, though stylistically later.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/kenzoubutu/kunisitei/0004.html |title=瀧山寺三門 |publisher=愛知県 |accessdate=2019-05-23}}* Wooden statues of Kannon Bosatsu, Brahma, and Indra: Created by Unkei and Tankei in 1201.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/choukoku/kunisitei/0306.html |title=木造観音菩薩・梵天・帝釈天立像 |publisher=愛知県 |accessdate=2013-05-31}} === Prefectural and Municipal Cultural Properties === Additional wooden statues, ritual implements, and documents are designated at the prefectural and municipal levels. == Gallery == {{Gallery|title=|width=200|height=150|lines=|File:Okazaki-Takisanji-10.jpg|Sanmon Gate (Taisho era)|File:Okazaki-Takisanji-6.jpg|Scene from Oni Festival (2018)|File:Okazaki-Takisanji-7.jpg|Scene from Oni Festival (2019)|File:Hiyoshi-Sannosha-Hoden-1.jpg|Hiyoshi Sannōsha Main Hall (2021)}} == Access == * From {{ill|Higashi Okazaki Station|ja|東岡崎駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Okazaki Station|qq=}}, take a Meitetsu bus to "Takisanji-shita" stop. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://takisanji.net Takisan-ji Official Website] {{translated page|ja|滝山寺|version=102899477|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Temples in Okazaki]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Tendai temples]] [[Category:Temples dedicated to Yakushi Nyorai]] [[Category:Prefectural cultural properties in Aichi]] }} [[sn:Takisanji]] [[ja:滝山寺]]

The temple, initially named "Kisshō-ji," was later abandoned and fell into ruin. En no Gyōja is associated with numerous temples across Japan, especially those related to mountain and water source worship. Takisan-ji is believed to have served as a center for mountain worship.In the {{ill|Hōan era|ja|保安 (元号)|lt=Hōan era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōan era|qq=}} (1120–1123), the temple was revived by Buddhist monk Eikyū (永救) of the {{ill|Tendai Sect|ja|天台宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tendai Sect|qq=}} with support from the local Mononobe clan. Eikyū built the Main Hall and secured patronage from the Fujiwara family, increasing the temple's influence. Eikyū, a monk from {{ill|Kaga Province|ja|加賀国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaga Province|qq=}}, was trained at {{ill|Mount Hiei|ja|比叡山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hiei|qq=}} before spreading the Tendai teachings in {{ill|Mikawa Province|ja|三河国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikawa Province|qq=}}.From the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian period|qq=}} to the early {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura period|qq=}}, head priest Kanden (寛伝, 1142–1205) strengthened the temple's ties with powerful figures such as {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoritomo|qq=}}, ensuring support from the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|ja|鎌倉幕府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura shogunate|qq=}}.{{Cite journal|和書 |author=Hira Masayuki |year=2017 |title=熱田大宮司家の寛伝僧都と源頼朝 ―瀧山寺・日光山・高野大鐘― |journal=人間文化研究 |volume=38 |pages=288-243 |url=http://id.nii.ac.jp/1455/00001180/}} Statues crafted by {{ill|Unkei|ja|運慶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unkei|qq=}} and {{ill|Tankei|ja|湛慶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tankei|qq=}} were created during this period, commemorating Yoritomo.戸澤剛{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.bunsin.org/三河ビュー/三河の文化を訪ねて-人物史/運慶作三尊像を滝山寺に誘った%e3%80%80式部僧都%e3%80%80寛伝/ |title=運慶作三尊像を滝山寺に誘った　式部僧都　寛伝 |publisher=愛知教育文化振興会 |accessdate=2019-5-23 }}During the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi period|qq=}}, the temple was supported by the {{ill|Ashikaga clan|ja|足利氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga clan|qq=}}. However, its influence waned during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}} due to shifting political priorities.[http://jlogos.com/ausp/word.html?id=7354185 阿知和郷(中世)]角川地名大辞典（旧地名）Under {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|ja|徳川家康|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa Ieyasu|qq=}}, the temple regained support and received 412 koku in stipends. {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa Iemitsu|qq=}} later established the adjacent Takisan Tōshō-gū.{{Cite book|和書|author= |year=1993-3-15|title=新編 岡崎市史 総集編 20|publisher=新編岡崎市史編さん委員会|page=231-232}}After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Restoration|qq=}}, the temple lost its landholdings and shRank in scale. Today, it continues as a significant cultural and religious site. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall: A 14th-century structure exemplifying Esoteric Buddhist architecture.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/kenzoubutu/kunisitei/0006.html |title=瀧山寺本堂 |publisher=愛知県 |accessdate=2013-05-31}}* Sanmon Gate: Dating to the Kamakura period, though stylistically later.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/kenzoubutu/kunisitei/0004.html |title=瀧山寺三門 |publisher=愛知県 |accessdate=2019-05-23}}* Wooden statues of Kannon Bosatsu, Brahma, and Indra: Created by Unkei and Tankei in 1201.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/choukoku/kunisitei/0306.html |title=木造観音菩薩・梵天・帝釈天立像 |publisher=愛知県 |accessdate=2013-05-31}} === Prefectural and Municipal Cultural Properties === Additional wooden statues, ritual implements, and documents are designated at the prefectural and municipal levels. == Gallery == {{Gallery|title=|width=200|height=150|lines=|File:Okazaki-Takisanji-10.jpg|Sanmon Gate (Taisho era)|File:Okazaki-Takisanji-6.jpg|Scene from Oni Festival (2018)|File:Okazaki-Takisanji-7.jpg|Scene from Oni Festival (2019)|File:Hiyoshi-Sannosha-Hoden-1.jpg|Hiyoshi Sannōsha Main Hall (2021)}} == Access == * From {{ill|Higashi Okazaki Station|ja|東岡崎駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Okazaki Station|qq=}}, take a Meitetsu bus to "Takisanji-shita" stop. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://takisanji.net Takisan-ji Official Website] {{translated page|ja|滝山寺|version=102899477|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Temples in Okazaki]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Tendai temples]] [[Category:Prefectural cultural properties in Aichi]] }} [[sn:Takisanji]] [[ja:滝山寺]]

revision.text:
{{日本の寺院 |名称 = 滝山寺 |image = [[File:Takisan-ji Temple Hondou, Yamagomori Taki-cho Okazaki 2022.jpg|270px]]
本堂（重要文化財）
（[[2022nd year ]]1月） |所在地 = [[愛知県]][[岡崎市]][[滝町 (岡崎市)|滝町]]山籠107 |位置 = {{Coord|34|59|17.37|N|137|12|20.26|E|region:JP-23_scale:20000_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=滝山寺}} |山号 = 吉祥陀羅尼山 |院号 = 薬樹王院 |宗派 = [[天台宗]] |本尊 = [[薬師如来]] |創建年 = 伝・[[Emperor Tenmu]]時代（[[673rd year ]] - [[686年]]） |開基 = 伝・[[役小角]] |別称 = |正式名 = 吉祥陀羅尼山藥樹王院瀧山寺 |文化財 = 本堂、三門、木造観音菩薩立像及梵天・帝釈天立像（重要文化財） |地図2 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|lat_deg=34|lat_min=59|lat_sec=17.37|lon_deg=137|lon_min=12|lon_sec=20.26|label=滝山寺|mark=Japanese Map symbol (Temple).svg|marksize=11|position=center|width=200|float=center}} |Name = Takisan-ji|image = ]][[File:Takisan-ji Temple Hondou, Yamagomori Taki-cho Okazaki 2022.jpg|270px]]
Main Hall (Important Cultural Property)
(]][[2022]] January)|Location = [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]][[:en:Okazaki, Aichi|Okazaki]]{{ill|Taki-cho|ja|滝町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taki-cho|qq=}} Yamagomori 107|Coordinates = {{Coord|34|59|17.37|N|137|12|20.26|E|region:JP-23_scale:20000_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=滝山寺}}|Mountain Name = Kisshōdarani-zan|Temple Name = Yakujūō-in|Sect = [[:en:Tiantai|Tendai Sect]]|Main image = [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]]|Founded = Traditionally during the era of Emperor Tenmu (673-686)|Founder = Traditionally [[:en:En no Gyōja|En no Gyōja]]|Alternate Names = |Official Name = 吉祥陀羅尼山藥樹王院瀧山寺|Cultural Properties = Main Hall, Sanmon Gate, Wooden Standing Statue of Kannon Bosatsu, and Standing Statues of Brahma and Indra (Important Cultural Properties)|Map = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|lat_deg=34|lat_min=59|lat_sec=17.37|lon_deg=137|lon_min=12|lon_sec=20.26|label=Takisan-ji|mark=Japanese Map symbol (Temple).svg|marksize=11|position=center|width=200|float=center}}}}'''Takisan-ji''' (滝山寺) is a [[:en:Tiantai|Tendai Sect]] Buddhist temple located in [[:en:Okazaki, Aichi|Okazaki]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. == Overview == === History === The mountain name is Kisshōdarani-zan, and the temple name is Yakujūō-in. The temple is renowned for the {{ill|Takisanji Oni Festival|ja|滝山寺鬼まつり|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takisanji Oni Festival|qq=}}.[[:en:Takisan Tōshō-gū|Takisan Tōshō-gū]] is located to the east, and the {{ill|Hiyoshi Sannōsha Main Hall|ja|滝山東照宮#日吉山王社本殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiyoshi Sannōsha Main Hall|qq=}} is to the north of the Main Hall.According to the "Takisan-ji Engi" (a historical record of the temple; existing manuscripts are from the early modern period), the temple was established during the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] by order of Emperor Tenmu. [[:en:En no Gyōja|En no Gyōja]], a legendary ascetic, enshrined a golden statue of [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]] found in the {{ill|Aoki River|ja|青木川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoki River|qq=}}. The temple, initially named "Kisshō-ji," was later abandoned and fell into ruin. En no Gyōja is associated with numerous temples across Japan, especially those related to mountain and water source worship. Takisan-ji is believed to have served as a center for mountain worship.In the [[:en:Hōan|Hōan era]] (1120–1123), the temple was revived by Buddhist monk Eikyū (永救) of the [[:en:Tiantai|Tendai Sect]] with support from the local Mononobe clan. Eikyū built the Main Hall and secured patronage from the Fujiwara family, increasing the temple's influence. Eikyū, a monk from [[:en:Kaga Province|Kaga Province]], was trained at [[:en:Mount Hiei|Mount Hiei]] before spreading the Tendai teachings in [[:en:Mikawa Province|Mikawa Province]].From the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] to the early [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], head priest Kanden (寛伝, 1142–1205) strengthened the temple's ties with powerful figures such as [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]], ensuring support from the [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura shogunate]].{{Cite journal|和書 |author=Hira Masayuki |year=2017 |title=熱田大宮司家の寛伝僧都と源頼朝 ―瀧山寺・日光山・高野大鐘― |journal=人間文化研究 |volume=38 |pages=288-243 |url=http://id.nii.ac.jp/1455/00001180/}} Statues crafted by [[:en:Unkei|Unkei]] and [[:en:Tankei|Tankei]] were created during this period, commemorating Yoritomo.戸澤剛{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.bunsin.org/三河ビュー/三河の文化を訪ねて-人物史/運慶作三尊像を滝山寺に誘った%e3%80%80式部僧都%e3%80%80寛伝/ |title=運慶作三尊像を滝山寺に誘った　式部僧都　寛伝 |publisher=愛知教育文化振興会 |accessdate=2019-5-23 }}During the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the temple was supported by the [[:en:Ashikaga clan|Ashikaga clan]]. However, its influence waned during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}} due to shifting political priorities.[http://jlogos.com/ausp/word.html?id=7354185 阿知和郷(中世)]角川地名大辞典（旧地名）Under [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]], the temple regained support and received 412 koku in stipends. [[:en:Tokugawa Iemitsu|Tokugawa Iemitsu]] later established the adjacent Takisan Tōshō-gū.{{Cite book|和書|author= |year=1993-3-15|title=新編 岡崎市史 総集編 20|publisher=新編岡崎市史編さん委員会|page=231-232}}After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the temple lost its landholdings and shRank in scale. Today, it continues as a significant cultural and religious site. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall: A 14th-century structure exemplifying Esoteric Buddhist architecture.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/kenzoubutu/kunisitei/0006.html |title=瀧山寺本堂 |publisher=愛知県 |accessdate=2013-05-31}}* Sanmon Gate: Dating to the Kamakura period, though stylistically later.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/kenzoubutu/kunisitei/0004.html |title=瀧山寺三門 |publisher=愛知県 |accessdate=2019-05-23}}* Wooden statues of Kannon Bosatsu, Brahma, and Indra: Created by Unkei and Tankei in 1201.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/choukoku/kunisitei/0306.html |title=木造観音菩薩・梵天・帝釈天立像 |publisher=愛知県 |accessdate=2013-05-31}} === Prefectural and Municipal Cultural Properties === Additional wooden statues, ritual implements, and documents are designated at the prefectural and municipal levels. == Gallery == {{Gallery|title=|width=200|height=150|lines=|File:Okazaki-Takisanji-10.jpg|Sanmon Gate (Taisho era)|File:Okazaki-Takisanji-6.jpg|Scene from Oni Festival (2018)|File:Okazaki-Takisanji-7.jpg|Scene from Oni Festival (2019)|File:Hiyoshi-Sannosha-Hoden-1.jpg|Hiyoshi Sannōsha Main Hall (2021)}} == Access == * From [[:en:Higashi Okazaki Station|Higashi Okazaki Station]], take a Meitetsu bus to "Takisanji-shita" stop. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://takisanji.net Takisan-ji Official Website] {{translated page|ja|滝山寺|version=102899477|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Temples in Okazaki]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Tendai temples]] [[Category:Prefectural cultural properties in Aichi]] }} [[sn:Takisanji]] [[ja:滝山寺]]

Main Hall (Important Cultural Property)

[[:en:Tokugawa Iemitsu|Tokugawa Iemitsu]] later established the adjacent Takisan Tōshō-gū.{{Cite book|和書|author= |year=1993-3-15|title=新編 岡崎市史 総集編 20|publisher=新編岡崎市史編さん委員会|page=231-232}}After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the temple lost its landholdings and shRank in scale. Today, it continues as a significant cultural and religious site. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall: A 14th-century structure exemplifying Esoteric Buddhist architecture.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/kenzoubutu/kunisitei/0006.html |title=瀧山寺本堂 |publisher=愛知県 |accessdate=2013-05-31}}* Sanmon Gate: Dating to the Kamakura period, though stylistically later.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/kenzoubutu/kunisitei/0004.html |title=瀧山寺三門 |publisher=愛知県 |accessdate=2019-05-23}}* Wooden statues of Kannon Bosatsu, Brahma, and Indra: Created by Unkei and Tankei in 1201.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/choukoku/kunisitei/0306.html |title=木造観音菩薩・梵天・帝釈天立像 |publisher=愛知県 |accessdate=2013-05-31}} === Prefectural and Municipal Cultural Properties === Additional wooden statues, ritual implements, and documents are designated at the prefectural and municipal levels. == Gallery == {{Gallery|title=|width=200|height=150|lines=|File:Okazaki-Takisanji-10.jpg|Sanmon Gate (Taisho era)|File:Okazaki-Takisanji-6.jpg|Scene from Oni Festival (2018)|File:Okazaki-Takisanji-7.jpg|Scene from Oni Festival (2019)|File:Hiyoshi-Sannosha-Hoden-1.jpg|Hiyoshi Sannōsha Main Hall (2021)}} == Access == * From [[:en:Higashi Okazaki Station|Higashi Okazaki Station]], take a Meitetsu bus to "Takisanji-shita" stop. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://takisanji.net Takisan-ji Official Website] {{translated page|ja|滝山寺|version=102899477|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Temples in Okazaki]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Tendai temples]] [[Category:Prefectural cultural properties in Aichi]] }} [[sn:Takisanji]] [[ja:滝山寺]] [[Category:Bettoji Temples]]

{{日本の寺院 |名称 = 滝山寺 |image = [[File:Takisan-ji Temple Hondou, Yamagomori Taki-cho Okazaki 2022.jpg|270px]]
本堂（重要文化財）
（[[2022nd year ]]1月） |所在地 = [[愛知県]][[岡崎市]][[滝町 (岡崎市)|滝町]]山籠107 |位置 = {{Coord|34|59|17.37|N|137|12|20.26|E|region:JP-23_scale:20000_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=滝山寺}} |山号 = 吉祥陀羅尼山 |院号 = 薬樹王院 |宗派 = [[天台宗]] |本尊 = [[薬師如来]] |創建年 = 伝・[[Emperor Tenmu]]時代（[[673rd year ]] - [[686年]]） |開基 = 伝・[[役小角]] |別称 = |正式名 = 吉祥陀羅尼山藥樹王院瀧山寺 |文化財 = 本堂、三門、木造観音菩薩立像及梵天・帝釈天立像（重要文化財） |地図2 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|lat_deg=34|lat_min=59|lat_sec=17.37|lon_deg=137|lon_min=12|lon_sec=20.26|label=滝山寺|mark=Japanese Map symbol (Temple).svg|marksize=11|position=center|width=200|float=center}} |Name = Takisan-ji|image = ]][[File:Takisan-ji Temple Hondou, Yamagomori Taki-cho Okazaki 2022.jpg|270px]]
Main Hall (Important Cultural Property)
(]][[2022]] January)|Location = [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]][[:en:Okazaki, Aichi|Okazaki]]{{ill|Taki-cho|ja|滝町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taki-cho|qq=}} Yamagomori 107|Coordinates = {{Coord|34|59|17.37|N|137|12|20.26|E|region:JP-23_scale:20000_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=滝山寺}}|Mountain Name = Kisshōdarani-zan|Temple Name = Yakujūō-in|Sect = [[:en:Tiantai|Tendai Sect]]|Main image = [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]]|Founded = Traditionally during the era of Emperor Tenmu (673-686)|Founder = Traditionally [[:en:En no Gyōja|En no Gyōja]]|Alternate Names = |Official Name = 吉祥陀羅尼山藥樹王院瀧山寺|Cultural Properties = Main Hall, Sanmon Gate, Wooden Standing Statue of Kannon Bosatsu, and Standing Statues of Brahma and Indra (Important Cultural Properties)|Map = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|lat_deg=34|lat_min=59|lat_sec=17.37|lon_deg=137|lon_min=12|lon_sec=20.26|label=Takisan-ji|mark=Japanese Map symbol (Temple).svg|marksize=11|position=center|width=200|float=center}}}}'''Takisan-ji''' (滝山寺) is a [[:en:Tiantai|Tendai Sect]] Buddhist temple located in [[:en:Okazaki, Aichi|Okazaki]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. == Overview == === History === The mountain name is Kisshōdarani-zan, and the temple name is Yakujūō-in. The temple is renowned for the {{ill|Takisanji Oni Festival|ja|滝山寺鬼まつり|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takisanji Oni Festival|qq=}}.[[:en:Takisan Tōshō-gū|Takisan Tōshō-gū]] is located to the east, and the {{ill|Hiyoshi Sannōsha Main Hall|ja|滝山東照宮#日吉山王社本殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiyoshi Sannōsha Main Hall|qq=}} is to the north of the Main Hall.According to the "Takisan-ji Engi" (a historical record of the temple; existing manuscripts are from the early modern period), the temple was established during the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] by order of Emperor Tenmu. [[:en:En no Gyōja|En no Gyōja]], a legendary ascetic, enshrined a golden statue of [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]] found in the {{ill|Aoki River|ja|青木川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoki River|qq=}}. The temple, initially named "Kisshō-ji," was later abandoned and fell into ruin. En no Gyōja is associated with numerous temples across Japan, especially those related to mountain and water source worship. Takisan-ji is believed to have served as a center for mountain worship.In the [[:en:Hōan|Hōan era]] (1120–1123), the temple was revived by Buddhist monk Eikyū (永救) of the [[:en:Tiantai|Tendai Sect]] with support from the local Mononobe clan. Eikyū built the Main Hall and secured patronage from the Fujiwara family, increasing the temple's influence. Eikyū, a monk from [[:en:Kaga Province|Kaga Province]], was trained at [[:en:Mount Hiei|Mount Hiei]] before spreading the Tendai teachings in [[:en:Mikawa Province|Mikawa Province]].From the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] to the early [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], head priest Kanden (寛伝, 1142–1205) strengthened the temple's ties with powerful figures such as [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]], ensuring support from the [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura shogunate]].{{Cite journal|和書 |author=Hira Masayuki |year=2017 |title=熱田大宮司家の寛伝僧都と源頼朝 ―瀧山寺・日光山・高野大鐘― |journal=人間文化研究 |volume=38 |pages=288-243 |url=http://id.nii.ac.jp/1455/00001180/}} Statues crafted by [[:en:Unkei|Unkei]] and [[:en:Tankei|Tankei]] were created during this period, commemorating Yoritomo.戸澤剛{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.bunsin.org/三河ビュー/三河の文化を訪ねて-人物史/運慶作三尊像を滝山寺に誘った%e3%80%80式部僧都%e3%80%80寛伝/ |title=運慶作三尊像を滝山寺に誘った　式部僧都　寛伝 |publisher=愛知教育文化振興会 |accessdate=2019-5-23 }}During the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the temple was supported by the [[:en:Ashikaga clan|Ashikaga clan]]. However, its influence waned during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}} due to shifting political priorities.[http://jlogos.com/ausp/word.html?id=7354185 阿知和郷(中世)]角川地名大辞典（旧地名）Under [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]], the temple regained support and received 412 koku in stipends. [[:en:Tokugawa Iemitsu|Tokugawa Iemitsu]] later established the adjacent Takisan Tōshō-gū.{{Cite book|和書|author= |year=1993-3-15|title=新編 岡崎市史 総集編 20|publisher=新編岡崎市史編さん委員会|page=231-232}}After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the temple lost its landholdings and shRank in scale. Today, it continues as a significant cultural and religious site. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall: A 14th-century structure exemplifying Esoteric Buddhist architecture.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/kenzoubutu/kunisitei/0006.html |title=瀧山寺本堂 |publisher=愛知県 |accessdate=2013-05-31}}* Sanmon Gate: Dating to the Kamakura period, though stylistically later.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/kenzoubutu/kunisitei/0004.html |title=瀧山寺三門 |publisher=愛知県 |accessdate=2019-05-23}}* Wooden statues of Kannon Bosatsu, Brahma, and Indra: Created by Unkei and Tankei in 1201.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/choukoku/kunisitei/0306.html |title=木造観音菩薩・梵天・帝釈天立像 |publisher=愛知県 |accessdate=2013-05-31}} === Prefectural and Municipal Cultural Properties === Additional wooden statues, ritual implements, and documents are designated at the prefectural and municipal levels. == Gallery == {{Gallery|title=|width=200|height=150|lines=|File:Okazaki-Takisanji-10.jpg|Sanmon Gate (Taisho era)|File:Okazaki-Takisanji-6.jpg|Scene from Oni Festival (2018)|File:Okazaki-Takisanji-7.jpg|Scene from Oni Festival (2019)|File:Hiyoshi-Sannosha-Hoden-1.jpg|Hiyoshi Sannōsha Main Hall (2021)}} == Access == * From [[:en:Higashi Okazaki Station|Higashi Okazaki Station]], take a Meitetsu bus to "Takisanji-shita" stop. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://takisanji.net Takisan-ji Official Website] {{translated page|ja|滝山寺|version=102899477|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Temples in Okazaki]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Tendai temples]] [[Category:Prefectural cultural properties in Aichi]] }} [[sn:Takisanji]] [[ja:滝山寺]] [[Category:Bettoji Temples]] [[Category:Tiantai temples]]

（[[2022nd year ]]1月） |所在地 = [[愛知県]][[岡崎市]][[滝町 (岡崎市)|滝町]]山籠107 |位置 = {{Coord|34|59|17.37|N|137|12|20.26|E|region:JP-23_scale:20000_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=滝山寺}} |山号 = 吉祥陀羅尼山 |院号 = 薬樹王院 |宗派 = [[天台宗]] |本尊 = [[薬師如来]] |創建年 = 伝・[[Emperor Tenmu]]時代（[[673rd year ]] - [[686年]]） |開基 = 伝・[[役小角]] |別称 = |正式名 = 吉祥陀羅尼山藥樹王院瀧山寺 |文化財 = 本堂、三門、木造観音菩薩立像及梵天・帝釈天立像（重要文化財） |地図2 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|lat_deg=34|lat_min=59|lat_sec=17.37|lon_deg=137|lon_min=12|lon_sec=20.26|label=滝山寺|mark=Japanese Map symbol (Temple).svg|marksize=11|position=center|width=200|float=center}} |Name = Takisan-ji|image = ]][[File:Takisan-ji Temple Hondou, Yamagomori Taki-cho Okazaki 2022.jpg|270px]]

(]][[2022]] January)|Location = [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]][[:en:Okazaki, Aichi|Okazaki]]{{ill|Taki-cho|ja|滝町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taki-cho|qq=}} Yamagomori 107|Coordinates = {{Coord|34|59|17.37|N|137|12|20.26|E|region:JP-23_scale:20000_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=滝山寺}}|Mountain Name = Kisshōdarani-zan|Temple Name = Yakujūō-in|Sect = [[:en:Tiantai|Tendai Sect]]|Main image = [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]]|Founded = Traditionally during the era of Emperor Tenmu (673-686)|Founder = Traditionally [[:en:En no Gyōja|En no Gyōja]]|Alternate Names = |Official Name = 吉祥陀羅尼山藥樹王院瀧山寺|Cultural Properties = Main Hall, Sanmon Gate, Wooden Standing Statue of Kannon Bosatsu, and Standing Statues of Brahma and Indra (Important Cultural Properties)|Map = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|lat_deg=34|lat_min=59|lat_sec=17.37|lon_deg=137|lon_min=12|lon_sec=20.26|label=Takisan-ji|mark=Japanese Map symbol (Temple).svg|marksize=11|position=center|width=200|float=center}}}}'''Takisan-ji''' (滝山寺) is a [[:en:Tiantai|Tendai Sect]] Buddhist temple located in [[:en:Okazaki, Aichi|Okazaki]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. == Overview == === History === The mountain name is Kisshōdarani-zan, and the temple name is Yakujūō-in. The temple is renowned for the {{ill|Takisanji Oni Festival|ja|滝山寺鬼まつり|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takisanji Oni Festival|qq=}}.[[:en:Takisan Tōshō-gū|Takisan Tōshō-gū]] is located to the east, and the {{ill|Hiyoshi Sannōsha Main Hall|ja|滝山東照宮#日吉山王社本殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiyoshi Sannōsha Main Hall|qq=}} is to the north of the Main Hall.According to the "Takisan-ji Engi" (a historical record of the temple; existing manuscripts are from the early modern period), the temple was established during the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] by order of Emperor Tenmu. [[:en:En no Gyōja|En no Gyōja]], a legendary ascetic, enshrined a golden statue of [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]] found in the {{ill|Aoki River|ja|青木川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoki River|qq=}}. The temple, initially named "Kisshō-ji," was later abandoned and fell into ruin. En no Gyōja is associated with numerous temples across Japan, especially those related to mountain and water source worship. Takisan-ji is believed to have served as a center for mountain worship.In the [[:en:Hōan|Hōan era]] (1120–1123), the temple was revived by Buddhist monk Eikyū (永救) of the [[:en:Tiantai|Tendai Sect]] with support from the local Mononobe clan. Eikyū built the Main Hall and secured patronage from the Fujiwara family, increasing the temple's influence. Eikyū, a monk from [[:en:Kaga Province|Kaga Province]], was trained at [[:en:Mount Hiei|Mount Hiei]] before spreading the Tendai teachings in [[:en:Mikawa Province|Mikawa Province]].From the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] to the early [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], head priest Kanden (寛伝, 1142–1205) strengthened the temple's ties with powerful figures such as [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]], ensuring support from the [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura shogunate]].{{Cite journal|和書 |author=Hira Masayuki |year=2017 |title=熱田大宮司家の寛伝僧都と源頼朝 ―瀧山寺・日光山・高野大鐘― |journal=人間文化研究 |volume=38 |pages=288-243 |url=http://id.nii.ac.jp/1455/00001180/}} Statues crafted by [[:en:Unkei|Unkei]] and [[:en:Tankei|Tankei]] were created during this period, commemorating Yoritomo.戸澤剛{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.bunsin.org/三河ビュー/三河の文化を訪ねて-人物史/運慶作三尊像を滝山寺に誘った%e3%80%80式部僧都%e3%80%80寛伝/ |title=運慶作三尊像を滝山寺に誘った　式部僧都　寛伝 |publisher=愛知教育文化振興会 |accessdate=2019-5-23 }}During the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the temple was supported by the [[:en:Ashikaga clan|Ashikaga clan]]. However, its influence waned during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}} due to shifting political priorities.[http://jlogos.com/ausp/word.html?id=7354185 阿知和郷(中世)]角川地名大辞典（旧地名）Under [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]], the temple regained support and received 412 koku in stipends. [[:en:Tokugawa Iemitsu|Tokugawa Iemitsu]] later established the adjacent Takisan Tōshō-gū.{{Cite book|和書|author= |year=1993-3-15|title=新編 岡崎市史 総集編 20|publisher=新編岡崎市史編さん委員会|page=231-232}}After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the temple lost its landholdings and shRank in scale. Today, it continues as a significant cultural and religious site. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall: A 14th-century structure exemplifying Esoteric Buddhist architecture.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/kenzoubutu/kunisitei/0006.html |title=瀧山寺本堂 |publisher=愛知県 |accessdate=2013-05-31}}* Sanmon Gate: Dating to the Kamakura period, though stylistically later.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/kenzoubutu/kunisitei/0004.html |title=瀧山寺三門 |publisher=愛知県 |accessdate=2019-05-23}}* Wooden statues of Kannon Bosatsu, Brahma, and Indra: Created by Unkei and Tankei in 1201.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/choukoku/kunisitei/0306.html |title=木造観音菩薩・梵天・帝釈天立像 |publisher=愛知県 |accessdate=2013-05-31}} === Prefectural and Municipal Cultural Properties === Additional wooden statues, ritual implements, and documents are designated at the prefectural and municipal levels. == Gallery == {{Gallery|title=|width=200|height=150|lines=|File:Okazaki-Takisanji-10.jpg|Sanmon Gate (Taisho era)|File:Okazaki-Takisanji-6.jpg|Scene from Oni Festival (2018)|File:Okazaki-Takisanji-7.jpg|Scene from Oni Festival (2019)|File:Hiyoshi-Sannosha-Hoden-1.jpg|Hiyoshi Sannōsha Main Hall (2021)}} == Access == * From [[:en:Higashi Okazaki Station|Higashi Okazaki Station]], take a Meitetsu bus to "Takisanji-shita" stop. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://takisanji.net Takisan-ji Official Website] {{translated page|ja|滝山寺|version=102899477|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Temples in Okazaki]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Tendai temples]] [[Category:Prefectural cultural properties in Aichi]] }} [[sn:Takisanji]] [[ja:滝山寺]] [[Category:Bettoji Temples]] [[Category:Tiantai temples]] [[Category:室町時代の建築]] [[Category:岡崎市の寺]]

However, its influence waned during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}} due to shifting political priorities.[http://jlogos.com/ausp/word.html?id=7354185 阿知和郷(中世)]角川地名大辞典（旧地名）Under [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]], the temple regained support and received 412 koku in stipends. [[:en:Tokugawa Iemitsu|Tokugawa Iemitsu]] later established the adjacent Takisan Tōshō-gū.{{Cite book|和書|author= |year=1993-3-15|title=新編 岡崎市史 総集編 20|publisher=新編岡崎市史編さん委員会|page=231-232}}After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the temple lost its landholdings and shRank in scale. Today, it continues as a significant cultural and religious site. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall: A 14th-century structure exemplifying Esoteric Buddhist architecture.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/kenzoubutu/kunisitei/0006.html |title=瀧山寺本堂 |publisher=愛知県 |accessdate=2013-05-31}}* Sanmon Gate: Dating to the Kamakura period, though stylistically later.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/kenzoubutu/kunisitei/0004.html |title=瀧山寺三門 |publisher=愛知県 |accessdate=2019-05-23}}* Wooden statues of Kannon Bosatsu, Brahma, and Indra: Created by Unkei and Tankei in 1201.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/choukoku/kunisitei/0306.html |title=木造観音菩薩・梵天・帝釈天立像 |publisher=愛知県 |accessdate=2013-05-31}} === Prefectural and Municipal Cultural Properties === Additional wooden statues, ritual implements, and documents are designated at the prefectural and municipal levels. == Gallery == {{Gallery|title=|width=200|height=150|lines=|File:Okazaki-Takisanji-10.jpg|Sanmon Gate (Taisho era)|File:Okazaki-Takisanji-6.jpg|Scene from Oni Festival (2018)|File:Okazaki-Takisanji-7.jpg|Scene from Oni Festival (2019)|File:Hiyoshi-Sannosha-Hoden-1.jpg|Hiyoshi Sannōsha Main Hall (2021)}} == Access == * From [[:en:Higashi Okazaki Station|Higashi Okazaki Station]], take a Meitetsu bus to "Takisanji-shita" stop. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://takisanji.net Takisan-ji Official Website] {{translated page|ja|滝山寺|version=102899477|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Temples in Okazaki]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Tendai temples]] [[Category:Prefectural cultural properties in Aichi]] }} [[sn:Takisanji]] [[ja:滝山寺]] [[Category:Bettoji Temples]] [[Category:Tiantai temples]] [[Category:室町時代の建築]] [[Category:岡崎市の寺]] [[Category:岡崎市の重要文化財]] [[Category:愛知県内の市町村指定有形文化財]]

revision.text:
{{日本の寺院 |名称 = 滝山寺 |image = [[File:Takisan-ji Temple Hondou, Yamagomori Taki-cho Okazaki 2022.jpg|270px]]
本堂（重要文化財）
（[[2022nd year ]]1月） |所在地 = [[愛知県]][[岡崎市]][[滝町 (岡崎市)|滝町]]山籠107 |位置 = {{Coord|34|59|17.37|N|137|12|20.26|E|region:JP-23_scale:20000_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=滝山寺}} |山号 = 吉祥陀羅尼山 |院号 = 薬樹王院 |宗派 = [[天台宗]] |本尊 = [[薬師如来]] |創建年 = 伝・[[Emperor Tenmu]]時代（[[673rd year ]] - [[686年]]） |開基 = 伝・[[役小角]] |別称 = |正式名 = 吉祥陀羅尼山藥樹王院瀧山寺 |文化財 = 本堂、三門、木造観音菩薩立像及梵天・帝釈天立像（重要文化財） |地図2 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|lat_deg=34|lat_min=59|lat_sec=17.37|lon_deg=137|lon_min=12|lon_sec=20.26|label=滝山寺|mark=Japanese Map symbol (Temple).svg|marksize=11|position=center|width=200|float=center}} |Name = Takisan-ji|image = ]][[File:Takisan-ji Temple Hondou, Yamagomori Taki-cho Okazaki 2022.jpg|270px]]
Main Hall (Important Cultural Property)
(]][[2022]] January)|Location = [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]][[:en:Okazaki, Aichi|Okazaki]]{{ill|Taki-cho|ja|滝町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taki-cho|qq=}} Yamagomori 107|Coordinates = {{Coord|34|59|17.37|N|137|12|20.26|E|region:JP-23_scale:20000_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=滝山寺}}|Mountain Name = Kisshōdarani-zan|Temple Name = Yakujūō-in|Sect = [[:en:Tiantai|Tendai Sect]]|Main image = [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]]|Founded = Traditionally during the era of Emperor Tenmu (673-686)|Founder = Traditionally [[:en:En no Gyōja|En no Gyōja]]|Alternate Names = |Official Name = 吉祥陀羅尼山藥樹王院瀧山寺|Cultural Properties = Main Hall, Sanmon Gate, Wooden Standing Statue of Kannon Bosatsu, and Standing Statues of Brahma and Indra (Important Cultural Properties)|Map = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|lat_deg=34|lat_min=59|lat_sec=17.37|lon_deg=137|lon_min=12|lon_sec=20.26|label=Takisan-ji|mark=Japanese Map symbol (Temple).svg|marksize=11|position=center|width=200|float=center}}}}'''Takisan-ji''' (滝山寺) is a [[:en:Tiantai|Tendai Sect]] Buddhist temple located in [[:en:Okazaki, Aichi|Okazaki]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. == Overview == === History === The mountain name is Kisshōdarani-zan, and the temple name is Yakujūō-in. The temple is renowned for the {{ill|Takisanji Oni Festival|ja|滝山寺鬼まつり|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takisanji Oni Festival|qq=}}.[[:en:Takisan Tōshō-gū|Takisan Tōshō-gū]] is located to the east, and the {{ill|Hiyoshi Sannōsha Main Hall|ja|滝山東照宮#日吉山王社本殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiyoshi Sannōsha Main Hall|qq=}} is to the north of the Main Hall.According to the "Takisan-ji Engi" (a historical record of the temple; existing manuscripts are from the early modern period), the temple was established during the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] by order of Emperor Tenmu. [[:en:En no Gyōja|En no Gyōja]], a legendary ascetic, enshrined a golden statue of [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]] found in the {{ill|Aoki River|ja|青木川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoki River|qq=}}. The temple, initially named "Kisshō-ji," was later abandoned and fell into ruin. En no Gyōja is associated with numerous temples across Japan, especially those related to mountain and water source worship. Takisan-ji is believed to have served as a center for mountain worship.In the [[:en:Hōan|Hōan era]] (1120–1123), the temple was revived by Buddhist monk Eikyū (永救) of the [[:en:Tiantai|Tendai Sect]] with support from the local Mononobe clan. Eikyū built the Main Hall and secured patronage from the Fujiwara family, increasing the temple's influence. Eikyū, a monk from [[:en:Kaga Province|Kaga Province]], was trained at [[:en:Mount Hiei|Mount Hiei]] before spreading the Tendai teachings in [[:en:Mikawa Province|Mikawa Province]].From the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] to the early [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], head priest Kanden (寛伝, 1142–1205) strengthened the temple's ties with powerful figures such as [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]], ensuring support from the [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura shogunate]].{{Cite journal|和書 |author=Hira Masayuki |year=2017 |title=熱田大宮司家の寛伝僧都と源頼朝 ―瀧山寺・日光山・高野大鐘― |journal=人間文化研究 |volume=38 |pages=288-243 |url=http://id.nii.ac.jp/1455/00001180/}} Statues crafted by [[:en:Unkei|Unkei]] and [[:en:Tankei|Tankei]] were created during this period, commemorating Yoritomo.戸澤剛{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.bunsin.org/三河ビュー/三河の文化を訪ねて-人物史/運慶作三尊像を滝山寺に誘った%e3%80%80式部僧都%e3%80%80寛伝/ |title=運慶作三尊像を滝山寺に誘った　式部僧都　寛伝 |publisher=愛知教育文化振興会 |accessdate=2019-5-23 }}During the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the temple was supported by the [[:en:Ashikaga clan|Ashikaga clan]]. However, its influence waned during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}} due to shifting political priorities.[http://jlogos.com/ausp/word.html?id=7354185 阿知和郷(中世)]角川地名大辞典（旧地名）Under [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]], the temple regained support and received 412 koku in stipends. [[:en:Tokugawa Iemitsu|Tokugawa Iemitsu]] later established the adjacent Takisan Tōshō-gū.{{Cite book|和書|author= |year=1993-3-15|title=新編 岡崎市史 総集編 20|publisher=新編岡崎市史編さん委員会|page=231-232}}After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the temple lost its landholdings and shRank in scale. Today, it continues as a significant cultural and religious site. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall: A 14th-century structure exemplifying Esoteric Buddhist architecture.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/kenzoubutu/kunisitei/0006.html |title=瀧山寺本堂 |publisher=愛知県 |accessdate=2013-05-31}}* Sanmon Gate: Dating to the Kamakura period, though stylistically later.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/kenzoubutu/kunisitei/0004.html |title=瀧山寺三門 |publisher=愛知県 |accessdate=2019-05-23}}* Wooden statues of Kannon Bosatsu, Brahma, and Indra: Created by Unkei and Tankei in 1201.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/choukoku/kunisitei/0306.html |title=木造観音菩薩・梵天・帝釈天立像 |publisher=愛知県 |accessdate=2013-05-31}} === Prefectural and Municipal Cultural Properties === Additional wooden statues, ritual implements, and documents are designated at the prefectural and municipal levels. == Gallery == {{Gallery|title=|width=200|height=150|lines=|File:Okazaki-Takisanji-10.jpg|Sanmon Gate (Taisho era)|File:Okazaki-Takisanji-6.jpg|Scene from Oni Festival (2018)|File:Okazaki-Takisanji-7.jpg|Scene from Oni Festival (2019)|File:Hiyoshi-Sannosha-Hoden-1.jpg|Hiyoshi Sannōsha Main Hall (2021)}} == Access == * From [[:en:Higashi Okazaki Station|Higashi Okazaki Station]], take a Meitetsu bus to "Takisanji-shita" stop. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://takisanji.net Takisan-ji Official Website] {{translated page|ja|滝山寺|version=102899477|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Temples in Okazaki]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Tendai temples]] [[Category:Prefectural cultural properties in Aichi]] }} [[sn:Takisanji]] [[ja:滝山寺]] [[Category:Bettoji Temples]] [[Category:Tiantai temples]] [[Category:室町時代の建築]] [[Category:岡崎市の寺]] [[Category:岡崎市の重要文化財]] [[Category:愛知県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:Aichi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]]

{{日本の寺院 |名称 = 滝山寺 |image = [[File:Takisan-ji Temple Hondou, Yamagomori Taki-cho Okazaki 2022.jpg|270px]]
本堂（重要文化財）
（[[2022nd year ]]1月） |所在地 = [[愛知県]][[岡崎市]][[滝町 (岡崎市)|滝町]]山籠107 |位置 = {{Coord|34|59|17.37|N|137|12|20.26|E|region:JP-23_scale:20000_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=滝山寺}} |山号 = 吉祥陀羅尼山 |院号 = 薬樹王院 |宗派 = [[天台宗]] |本尊 = [[薬師如来]] |創建年 = 伝・[[Emperor Tenmu]]時代（[[673rd year ]] - [[686年]]） |開基 = 伝・[[役小角]] |別称 = |正式名 = 吉祥陀羅尼山藥樹王院瀧山寺 |文化財 = 本堂、三門、木造観音菩薩立像及梵天・帝釈天立像（重要文化財） |地図2 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|lat_deg=34|lat_min=59|lat_sec=17.37|lon_deg=137|lon_min=12|lon_sec=20.26|label=滝山寺|mark=Japanese Map symbol (Temple).svg|marksize=11|position=center|width=200|float=center}} |Name = Takisan-ji|image = ]][[File:Takisan-ji Temple Hondou, Yamagomori Taki-cho Okazaki 2022.jpg|270px]]
Main Hall (Important Cultural Property)
(]][[2022]] January)|Location = [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]][[:en:Okazaki, Aichi|Okazaki]]{{ill|Taki-cho|ja|滝町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taki-cho|qq=}} Yamagomori 107|Coordinates = {{Coord|34|59|17.37|N|137|12|20.26|E|region:JP-23_scale:20000_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=滝山寺}}|Mountain Name = Kisshōdarani-zan|Temple Name = Yakujūō-in|Sect = [[:en:Tiantai|Tendai Sect]]|Main image = [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]]|Founded = Traditionally during the era of Emperor Tenmu (673-686)|Founder = Traditionally [[:en:En no Gyōja|En no Gyōja]]|Alternate Names = |Official Name = 吉祥陀羅尼山藥樹王院瀧山寺|Cultural Properties = Main Hall, Sanmon Gate, Wooden Standing Statue of Kannon Bosatsu, and Standing Statues of Brahma and Indra (Important Cultural Properties)|Map = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|lat_deg=34|lat_min=59|lat_sec=17.37|lon_deg=137|lon_min=12|lon_sec=20.26|label=Takisan-ji|mark=Japanese Map symbol (Temple).svg|marksize=11|position=center|width=200|float=center}}}}'''Takisan-ji''' (滝山寺) is a [[:en:Tiantai|Tendai Sect]] Buddhist temple located in [[:en:Okazaki, Aichi|Okazaki]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. == Overview == === History === The mountain name is Kisshōdarani-zan, and the temple name is Yakujūō-in. The temple is renowned for the {{ill|Takisanji Oni Festival|ja|滝山寺鬼まつり|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takisanji Oni Festival|qq=}}.[[:en:Takisan Tōshō-gū|Takisan Tōshō-gū]] is located to the east, and the {{ill|Hiyoshi Sannōsha Main Hall|ja|滝山東照宮#日吉山王社本殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiyoshi Sannōsha Main Hall|qq=}} is to the north of the Main Hall.According to the "Takisan-ji Engi" (a historical record of the temple; existing manuscripts are from the early modern period), the temple was established during the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] by order of Emperor Tenmu. [[:en:En no Gyōja|En no Gyōja]], a legendary ascetic, enshrined a golden statue of [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]] found in the {{ill|Aoki River|ja|青木川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoki River|qq=}}. The temple, initially named "Kisshō-ji," was later abandoned and fell into ruin. En no Gyōja is associated with numerous temples across Japan, especially those related to mountain and water source worship. Takisan-ji is believed to have served as a center for mountain worship.In the [[:en:Hōan|Hōan era]] (1120–1123), the temple was revived by Buddhist monk Eikyū (永救) of the [[:en:Tiantai|Tendai Sect]] with support from the local Mononobe clan. Eikyū built the Main Hall and secured patronage from the Fujiwara family, increasing the temple's influence. Eikyū, a monk from [[:en:Kaga Province|Kaga Province]], was trained at [[:en:Mount Hiei|Mount Hiei]] before spreading the Tendai teachings in [[:en:Mikawa Province|Mikawa Province]].From the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] to the early [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], head priest Kanden (寛伝, 1142–1205) strengthened the temple's ties with powerful figures such as [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]], ensuring support from the [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura shogunate]].{{Cite journal|和書 |author=Hira Masayuki |year=2017 |title=熱田大宮司家の寛伝僧都と源頼朝 ―瀧山寺・日光山・高野大鐘― |journal=人間文化研究 |volume=38 |pages=288-243 |url=http://id.nii.ac.jp/1455/00001180/}} Statues crafted by [[:en:Unkei|Unkei]] and [[:en:Tankei|Tankei]] were created during this period, commemorating Yoritomo.戸澤剛{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.bunsin.org/三河ビュー/三河の文化を訪ねて-人物史/運慶作三尊像を滝山寺に誘った%e3%80%80式部僧都%e3%80%80寛伝/ |title=運慶作三尊像を滝山寺に誘った　式部僧都　寛伝 |publisher=愛知教育文化振興会 |accessdate=2019-5-23 }}During the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the temple was supported by the [[:en:Ashikaga clan|Ashikaga clan]]. However, its influence waned during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}} due to shifting political priorities.[http://jlogos.com/ausp/word.html?id=7354185 阿知和郷(中世)]角川地名大辞典（旧地名）Under [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]], the temple regained support and received 412 koku in stipends. [[:en:Tokugawa Iemitsu|Tokugawa Iemitsu]] later established the adjacent Takisan Tōshō-gū.{{Cite book|和書|author= |year=1993-3-15|title=新編 岡崎市史 総集編 20|publisher=新編岡崎市史編さん委員会|page=231-232}}After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the temple lost its landholdings and shRank in scale. Today, it continues as a significant cultural and religious site. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall: A 14th-century structure exemplifying Esoteric Buddhist architecture.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/kenzoubutu/kunisitei/0006.html |title=瀧山寺本堂 |publisher=愛知県 |accessdate=2013-05-31}}* Sanmon Gate: Dating to the Kamakura period, though stylistically later.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/kenzoubutu/kunisitei/0004.html |title=瀧山寺三門 |publisher=愛知県 |accessdate=2019-05-23}}* Wooden statues of Kannon Bosatsu, Brahma, and Indra: Created by Unkei and Tankei in 1201.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/choukoku/kunisitei/0306.html |title=木造観音菩薩・梵天・帝釈天立像 |publisher=愛知県 |accessdate=2013-05-31}} === Prefectural and Municipal Cultural Properties === Additional wooden statues, ritual implements, and documents are designated at the prefectural and municipal levels. == Gallery == {{Gallery|title=|width=200|height=150|lines=|File:Okazaki-Takisanji-10.jpg|Sanmon Gate (Taisho era)|File:Okazaki-Takisanji-6.jpg|Scene from Oni Festival (2018)|File:Okazaki-Takisanji-7.jpg|Scene from Oni Festival (2019)|File:Hiyoshi-Sannosha-Hoden-1.jpg|Hiyoshi Sannōsha Main Hall (2021)}} == Access == * From [[:en:Higashi Okazaki Station|Higashi Okazaki Station]], take a Meitetsu bus to "Takisanji-shita" stop. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://takisanji.net Takisan-ji Official Website] {{translated page|ja|滝山寺|version=102899477|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Temples in Okazaki]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Tendai temples]] [[Category:Prefectural cultural properties in Aichi]] }} [[sn:Takisanji]] [[ja:滝山寺]] [[Category:Bettoji Temples]] [[Category:Tiantai temples]] [[Category:室町時代の建築]] [[Category:岡崎市の寺]] [[Category:岡崎市の重要文化財]] [[Category:愛知県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:Aichi Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Temples of Bhaiṣajyaguru]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]]

Eikyū, a monk from [[:en:Kaga Province|Kaga Province]], was trained at [[:en:Mount Hiei|Mount Hiei]] before spreading the Tendai teachings in [[:en:Mikawa Province|Mikawa Province]].From the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] to the early [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], head priest Kanden (寛伝, 1142–1205) strengthened the temple's ties with powerful figures such as [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]], ensuring support from the [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura shogunate]].{{Cite journal|和書 |author=Hira Masayuki |year=2017 |title=熱田大宮司家の寛伝僧都と源頼朝 ―瀧山寺・日光山・高野大鐘― |journal=人間文化研究 |volume=38 |pages=288-243 |url=http://id.nii.ac.jp/1455/00001180/}} Statues crafted by [[:en:Unkei|Unkei]] and [[:en:Tankei|Tankei]] were created during this period, commemorating Yoritomo.戸澤剛{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.bunsin.org/三河ビュー/三河の文化を訪ねて-人物史/運慶作三尊像を滝山寺に誘った%e3%80%80式部僧都%e3%80%80寛伝/ |title=運慶作三尊像を滝山寺に誘った　式部僧都　寛伝 |publisher=愛知教育文化振興会 |accessdate=2019-5-23 }}During the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the temple was supported by the [[:en:Ashikaga clan|Ashikaga clan]]. However, its influence waned during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}} due to shifting political priorities.[http://jlogos.com/ausp/word.html?id=7354185 阿知和郷(中世)]角川地名大辞典（旧地名）Under [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]], the temple regained support and received 412 koku in stipends. [[:en:Tokugawa Iemitsu|Tokugawa Iemitsu]] later established the adjacent Takisan Tōshō-gū.{{Cite book|和書|author= |year=1993-3-15|title=新編 岡崎市史 総集編 20|publisher=新編岡崎市史編さん委員会|page=231-232}}After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the temple lost its landholdings and shRank in scale. Today, it continues as a significant cultural and religious site. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall: A 14th-century structure exemplifying Esoteric Buddhist architecture.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/kenzoubutu/kunisitei/0006.html |title=瀧山寺本堂 |publisher=愛知県 |accessdate=2013-05-31}}* Sanmon Gate: Dating to the Kamakura period, though stylistically later.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/kenzoubutu/kunisitei/0004.html |title=瀧山寺三門 |publisher=愛知県 |accessdate=2019-05-23}}* Wooden statues of Kannon Bosatsu, Brahma, and Indra: Created by Unkei and Tankei in 1201.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/choukoku/kunisitei/0306.html |title=木造観音菩薩・梵天・帝釈天立像 |publisher=愛知県 |accessdate=2013-05-31}} === Prefectural and Municipal Cultural Properties === Additional wooden statues, ritual implements, and documents are designated at the prefectural and municipal levels. == Gallery == {{Gallery|title=|width=200|height=150|lines=|File:Okazaki-Takisanji-10.jpg|Sanmon Gate (Taisho era)|File:Okazaki-Takisanji-6.jpg|Scene from Oni Festival (2018)|File:Okazaki-Takisanji-7.jpg|Scene from Oni Festival (2019)|File:Hiyoshi-Sannosha-Hoden-1.jpg|Hiyoshi Sannōsha Main Hall (2021)}} == Access == * From [[:en:Higashi Okazaki Station|Higashi Okazaki Station]], take a Meitetsu bus to "Takisanji-shita" stop. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://takisanji.net Takisan-ji Official Website] {{translated page|ja|滝山寺|version=102899477|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Temples in Okazaki]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Tendai temples]] [[Category:Prefectural cultural properties in Aichi]] }} [[sn:Takisanji]]
